objective: Add Supabase Realtime subscription on the agency side so agencies see bid accept/reject/counter events without polling.
inputs: src/backend/services/realtime.ts — subscribeToCareContractBids()
Agency marketplace/bid pages (agency dashboard or bid list view)
Existing guardian-side pattern in GuardianMarketplaceHubPage.tsx and BidReviewPage.tsx
constraints: USE_SUPABASE guard — no-op in mock mode
Must not duplicate channels if the component re-renders
Unsubscribe on unmount
deliverables: Agency bid list page subscribes to care_contract_bids for the agency's own bids
UI updates (refetch or toast) when guardian accepts, rejects, or counters
definition_of_done: Agency logs in, submits a bid; guardian accepts → agency page updates without manual refresh
No console errors on mount/unmount cycle
Works in both Supabase and mock mode (mock = no-op)
validation_steps: Open two browser tabs: guardian + agency
Agency submits bid → guardian sees toast (already done)
Guardian accepts bid → agency page reflects updated bid status
failure_conditions: Channel leak: subscribing without cleanup on unmount
Subscribing when USE_SUPABASE is false and crashing
Showing stale data after accept/reject

objective: Schedule the marketplace-expire-bids Edge Function to run periodically so stale bids auto-expire.
inputs: supabase/functions/marketplace-expire-bids/index.ts (already deployed)
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
Supabase Dashboard → Database → Extensions → pg_cron (or external scheduler)
constraints: Must use Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY auth
Interval: every 15–60 minutes (pick based on bid expiry window — currently 12–24h)
Idempotent: multiple runs don't double-expire
deliverables: pg_cron job or external cron calling POST to marketplace-expire-bids endpoint
Verified: bids with expires_at in the past move to 'expired' status
definition_of_done: Create a bid with expires_at = now() - interval '1 hour'
Wait for cron tick (or trigger manually)
Bid status is 'expired'
validation_steps: Insert test bid with past expires_at via SQL editor
Invoke function manually: curl -X POST with Bearer token
Confirm bid status changed to 'expired'
Confirm cron schedule is registered (select * from cron.job)
failure_conditions: Cron never fires (misconfigured schedule or missing extension)
Auth fails (wrong key or missing Bearer prefix)
Function crashes on empty result set

objective: Manually verify RLS policies on care_contracts and care_contract_bids by testing as guardian, agency, and unauthenticated roles.
inputs: Live Supabase project with deployed RLS policies (cc_select_marketplace, cc_insert_own, cc_update_own, cc_delete_own, ccb_select, ccb_insert, ccb_update, ccb_delete)
At least one guardian account and one agency account
constraints: Do not disable RLS during testing
Test with real Supabase client (browser console or Postman with JWT)
deliverables: Test report: which queries return expected rows, which are correctly blocked
Fix any policy that leaks data or blocks legitimate access
definition_of_done: Guardian sees own requests + published listings only
Agency sees published requests + own offers only
Bids visible only to request owner + bidding agency
Bid INSERT blocked if contract is not published/bidding/matched
Unauthenticated user sees nothing (RLS blocks all)
validation_steps: As guardian: .from('care_contracts').select('*') → only own + published
As agency: .from('care_contracts').select('*') → published requests + own offers
As guardian: .from('care_contract_bids').select('*') → only bids on own requests
As agency: .from('care_contract_bids').select('*') → only own bids
As agency: insert bid on a draft contract → expect RLS error
As agency: insert bid on a published contract → expect success
failure_conditions: Guardian can see another guardian's draft requests
Agency can see bids from other agencies on requests they haven't bid on
Bid INSERT succeeds on a draft or cancelled contract
Any query returns rows belonging to another user

objective: Build the bill generation → payment confirmation → receipt verification flow using existing invoices + payment_proofs tables. No external gateway — payer confirms manual payment (bank/bKash/Nagad) with reference number, receiver confirms receipt.
inputs: Existing DB tables: invoices (from/to party, status, line items), payment_proofs (method, reference_number, screenshot_url, status), invoice_line_items
Invoice statuses: unpaid → proof_submitted → verified → paid
Payment proof methods: bkash, nagad, rocket, bank_transfer, cash
src/backend/services/billing.service.ts (existing service)
src/backend/models/billing.model.ts (existing model)
constraints: No external payment gateway integration — manual payment confirmation only
Every entity (guardian, agency, caregiver) must have a Bills & Payments section
Invoice generated after care contract reaches 'completed' status
Payer submits: amount, payment method (bKash/Nagad/Rocket/bank/cash), reference number, optional screenshot
Receiver gets notification → confirms receipt → invoice moves to 'verified' then 'paid'
Wallets table does NOT exist yet — decide whether to add a ledger or rely on invoice history as the financial record
deliverables: Bill generation: system creates invoice row when contract completes (or manually by agency)
Payer view: see unpaid bills, submit payment proof (amount, method, reference number, screenshot)
Receiver view: see pending proofs, confirm or reject receipt
Notification: real-time notification to receiver when proof is submitted, to payer when receipt is confirmed/rejected
Bills & Payments page accessible from guardian, agency, and caregiver dashboards
RLS: invoice parties only; payment proof parties only (already in schema)
definition_of_done: Contract completes → invoice auto-generated with correct line items and total
Guardian opens Bills page → sees unpaid invoice → submits payment proof with bKash reference
Agency gets notification → opens Bills page → sees proof → clicks 'Confirm Receipt'
Invoice status: unpaid → proof_submitted → verified
Both parties can view payment history
validation_steps: Complete a care contract → verify invoice row created in invoices table
As payer: submit payment proof → verify payment_proofs row with correct method and reference
As receiver: confirm receipt → verify invoice status updated to 'verified'
Verify notification sent on proof submission and on receipt confirmation
Verify RLS: payer cannot see other users' invoices; receiver cannot see other receivers' proofs
failure_conditions: Invoice generated with wrong party IDs or amounts
Payment proof accepted without required fields (method, reference_number)
Receiver can confirm receipt on someone else's invoice
No notification sent — receiver never knows payment was submitted
Duplicate invoices generated for same completed contract

product_decisions.wallet_ledger: Decide: add a wallets table for running balance per entity, or use invoice history only. Recommendation: start with invoice history — wallets add complexity (credits, debits, reconciliation) that isn't needed until you have volume.
product_decisions.auto_vs_manual_invoice: Decide: auto-generate invoice on contract completion, or let agency create manually. Recommendation: auto-generate with manual override (agency can edit line items before sending).
product_decisions.payment_methods: Supported: bKash, Nagad, Rocket, bank_transfer, cash (already in payment_proofs CHECK constraint).

objective: Replace the matching-run stub with real scoring logic using a config-driven weights JSON file. Rank agencies by city overlap, category fit, and price range.
inputs: supabase/functions/matching-run/index.ts (stub deployed)
care_contracts table (request: city, categories, pricing)
care_contracts table (offers: agency packages with city, category, base_price)
Spec: packages-1.md §1.4 Matching Engine
constraints: Run server-side only (Edge Function or DB function) — not in browser
Respect RLS: caller must own the contract or be admin
Return top N matches with explainable scores
Weights and signals must be in a JSON config file (not hardcoded) so they can be tuned without code changes
deliverables: src/config/matching-weights.json — signal weights config: { city_match: 40, category_overlap: 35, price_fit: 25 }
matching-run Edge Function reads weights from config and scores agencies
Score formula: city_score (exact match = full weight, else 0), category_score (overlap ratio * weight), price_score (within budget = full weight, within 20% overshoot = half, else 0)
Response includes per-agency score breakdown with reasons
Optional: persist scores in a match_scores table for caching
definition_of_done: POST /matching-run with a published request contract_id → returns ranked agencies with scores
Agencies in same city score higher than those outside
Agencies matching all requested categories score higher
Scores are deterministic for same input data
Changing matching-weights.json values changes scores without code deployment
validation_steps: Create a request in Dhaka for elderly + chronic care
Create 3 agency packages: Dhaka+elderly, Dhaka+baby, Chittagong+elderly
Call matching-run → Dhaka+elderly ranks first
Verify score breakdown in response
Change city_match weight in JSON → re-run → scores shift proportionally
failure_conditions: Returning agencies the caller can't see (RLS bypass)
Non-deterministic scores on same data
Timeout on large dataset (optimize query if > 2s)
Missing score explanation (opaque ranking)
Hardcoded weights that require code changes to tune

objective: Write an integration test against the real Supabase DB proving the lifecycle trigger rejects invalid transitions.
inputs: Linked Supabase project with lifecycle trigger deployed
Vitest or Playwright test harness
Test user credentials (guardian)
constraints: Test against live DB (not mock)
Clean up test data after each run
Must not interfere with production data
deliverables: Test file: insert draft contract, try update to 'active' → expect Postgres error
Test: draft → published → bidding → locked (valid path) → succeeds
Test: insert with status = 'active' → expect INSERT trigger rejection
definition_of_done: Test passes in CI with USE_SUPABASE=true
Invalid transitions produce check_violation error
Valid transitions succeed without error
validation_steps: npx vitest run <test-file> with live Supabase credentials
All assertions pass
No leftover test rows in care_contracts
failure_conditions: Test passes even when trigger is missing (false positive)
Test data leaks into production
Flaky due to network or auth timeout
